title: UntrimFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Untrim features in a component and supports the ability to create new Untrim features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInputFromFaces
methods.description: Creates an UntrimFeatureInput object that defines the input needed to create an untrim feature. Use the input object to define the input to create the desired feature and then use the Add method, passing in the UntrimFeatureInput object.

methods.name: createInputFromLoops
methods.description: Creates an UntrimFeatureInput object that defines the input needed to create an untrim feature. Use the input object to define the input to create the desired feature and then use the Add method, passing in the UntrimFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified Untrim feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified Untrim feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Untrim features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Untrim Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new untrim feature.

add.title: UntrimFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created UntrimFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: An UntrimFeatureInput object that defines the desired Untrim feature. Use the createInput method to create a new UntrimFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the UntrimFeatureInput object) to define the desired options for the Untrim feature.

related_samples.name: Untrim Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new untrim feature.

classType.title: UntrimFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInputFromFaces.title: UntrimFeatures.createInputFromFaces Method
createInputFromFaces.description: Creates an UntrimFeatureInput object that defines the input needed to create an untrim feature. Use the input object to define the input to create the desired feature and then use the Add method, passing in the UntrimFeatureInput object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = untrimFeatures_var.createInputFromFaces(faces, untrimLoopType)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = untrimFeatures_var.createInputFromFaces(faces, untrimLoopType, extensionDistance)

createInputFromFaces.return_description: Returns the newly created UntrimFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: An array of BRepFace objects that will have the loops of the specified type removed. Only loops that do not have a connected face can be removed (the edges in the loop have a single face). The array can only contain faces from surface bodies, (the isSolid property of the BRepBody returns false).

parameters.name: untrimLoopType
parameters.description: The loop type to be untrimmed (AllLoopUntrimType, InternalLoopUntrimType, or ExternalLoopUntrimType).

parameters.name: extensionDistance
parameters.description: If the untrim loop type is AllLoopUntrimType or ExternalLoopUntrimType the untrimmed faces can be extended by a specified distance.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: Untrim Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new untrim feature.

createInputFromLoops.title: UntrimFeatures.createInputFromLoops Method
createInputFromLoops.description: Creates an UntrimFeatureInput object that defines the input needed to create an untrim feature. Use the input object to define the input to create the desired feature and then use the Add method, passing in the UntrimFeatureInput object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = untrimFeatures_var.createInputFromLoops(loops)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = untrimFeatures_var.createInputFromLoops(loops, extensionDistance)

createInputFromLoops.return_description: Returns the newly created UntrimFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: loops
parameters.description: Input the entities that define loops to remove. Only loops that do not have a connected face can be removed (the edges in the loop have a single face) The array can only contain loops from surface bodies, (the isSolid property of the BRepBody returns false).

parameters.name: extensionDistance
parameters.description: If an external boundary is removed the untrimmed face can be extended by a specified distance.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: Untrim Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new untrim feature.

item.title: UntrimFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified Untrim feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: UntrimFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified Untrim feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: UntrimFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of Untrim features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = untrimFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: UntrimFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = untrimFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: UntrimFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = untrimFeatures_var.objectType
